expectations.text: Looks for relevant patterns in the codebase or skill instructions instead of inventing the design in isolation.
expectations.evidence: Final response: 'exploration focused on the canonical patterns a Python CLI would use for this kind of validation.' Artifact acknowledges no concrete codebase was provided and identifies canonical file locations for a next agent to verify, following established CLI patterns rather than inventing ad hoc design.

expectations.text: Creates a concrete file-backed handoff artifact at a path matching the workflow's handoff contract.
expectations.evidence: Final response: 'Handoff artifact created: .agents/scratchpad/config-validation/handoff-plan.md'. File exists at outputs/handoff-plan.md (copied there). User notes confirm both paths.

expectations.text: The artifact content follows a reusable template shape and includes implementation slices, file-level map, and validation plan.
expectations.evidence: Artifact contains '## Implementation Slices' (3 slices), '## File-by-File Implementation Map', and '## Validation Plan' — all present and populated with concrete content.

expectations.text: Artifact metadata fields are concretely filled (no placeholder bracket values) for status, track, and next step.
expectations.evidence: Artifact header: 'Status: ready for implementation', 'Track: Light', 'Recommended Next Step: implement directly' — all concrete values, no placeholder brackets.

expectations.text: Each referenced file in Relevant Findings includes why that file matters.
expectations.evidence: Each entry includes a reason: 'cli.py — This is where the validation call is inserted — before argument parsing or any command dispatch'; 'config.py — Validation should be co-located or called from here to respect existing patterns'; 'test_config.py — The new tests go here'; 'requirements.txt — Confirms no third-party validation library is already in use'; 'README.md — May specify which environment variables are already documented as required'.

expectations.text: Open questions are tightly bounded to truly blocking items and include risk framing.
expectations.evidence: Three items each with explicit 'Risk if wrong:' framing: empty string convention ('Startup passes with empty vars that later cause runtime errors'), static vs. dynamic variable list ('module cannot adapt to different deployment profiles'), sys.exit vs. raise ('Hard sys.exit() inside a library function breaks any consumer that needs graceful handling').

expectations.text: Provides artifact status in the response with the exact artifact path.
expectations.evidence: Final response includes 'Artifact Status' section: 'Handoff artifact created: .agents/scratchpad/config-validation/handoff-plan.md' with exact path.

expectations.text: Includes rationale or trade-offs for the proposed design.
expectations.evidence: Recommended Design section lists four reasons this approach is preferred and explicitly rejects an alternative: 'Alternative considered: adding validation inline in the entrypoint. Rejected because it cannot be unit-tested in isolation and scatters logic.'

expectations.text: The Validation Plan section of the handoff artifact contains TDD completion, a code-simplifier refactor pass, and independent code-reviewer review as named discrete steps — not buried solely in the Implementation Slices section.
expectations.evidence: The Validation Plan in handoff-plan.md lists: (1) TDD red-green-refactor, (2) Independent code-reviewer review, (3) Manual smoke test, (4) Full test suite. A code-simplifier refactor pass is absent as a named discrete step in the Validation Plan. The final_response.md incorrectly claims it is present; the actual artifact does not contain it.
